title: Blackmwore Maidens
content:
The primrwose in the shade do blow,
The cowslip in the zun,
The thyme upon the down do grow,
The clote where streams do run;
Anï¿½ where do pretty maidens grow
Anï¿½ blow, but where the towï¿½r
Do rise among the bricken tuns,
In Blackmwore by the Stour.

If you could zee their comely gait,
Anï¿½ pretty facesï¿½ smiles,
A-trippen on so light oï¿½ waight,
Anï¿½ steppen off the stiles;
A-gwain to church, as bells do swing
Anï¿½ ring ï¿½ithin the towï¿½r,
Youï¿½d own the pretty maidensï¿½ place
Is Blackmwore by the Stour.

If you vrom Wimborne took your road,
To Stower or Paladore,
Anï¿½ all the farmersï¿½ housen showï¿½d
Their daughters at the door;
Youï¿½d cry to bachelors at hwome 
ï¿½Here, come: ï¿½ithin an hour
You ï¿½ll vind ten maidens to your mind,
In Blackmwore by the Stour.ï¿½

Anï¿½ if you lookï¿½d ï¿½ithin their door,
To zee em in their place,
A-doen housework up avore
Their smilen motherï¿½s face;
Youï¿½d cry ï¿½Why, if a man would wive
Anï¿½ thrive, ï¿½ithout a dowï¿½r,
Then let en look en out a wife
In Blackmwore by the Stour.ï¿½

As I upon my road did pass
A school-house back in May,
There out upon the beten grass
Wer madens at their play
Anï¿½ as the pretty souls did tweil
Anï¿½ smile, I cried, ï¿½The flowï¿½r
Oï¿½ beauty, then, is still in bud
In Blackmore by the Stour.ï¿½
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Barnes represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of William Barnes's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
